perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the disclosure (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed): (a) that the Discussions and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the disclosure (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed), unless either party deems it necessary to disclose such information to potential investors.: (a) that the Discussions and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 7 originally prevents disclosing discussion to the public with the exception of what's required by law. The introduced clause that states "unless either party deems it necessary to disclose such information to potential investors" contradicts with the first statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information Regarding Existence of Discussions and Negotiations. During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the disclosure (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed), unless either party deems it necessary to disclose such information to potential investors.: (a) that the Discussions and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure of discussions and negotiations except as required by law. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing disclosure to potential investors, which contradicts the initial restriction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, further highlighting the internal contradiction within the document after the modification. The explanation also supports this analysis.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the execution of this Agreement does not in any way constitute a binding commitment on the part of either party to complete the Discussions or Negotiations or enter into any joint business initiative(s) or any other resulting transaction(s) with the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that the execution of this Agreement does in some way constitute a binding commitment on the part of either party to complete the Discussions or Negotiations or enter into any joint business initiative(s) or any other resulting transaction(s) with the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation in Section 9 creates a direct contradiction by changing "does not in any way constitute a binding commitment" to "does in some way constitute a binding commitment". The original statement was clear, but the change implies an obligation to complete discussions, negations, etc.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Commitment to Enter Into Any Other Agreement(s). The parties agree that the execution of this Agreement does in some way constitute a binding commitment on the part of either party to complete the Discussions or Negotiations or enter into any joint business initiative(s) or any other resulting transaction(s) with the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the execution of the agreement 'does in some way constitute a binding commitment', while the contradicted text states the exact same thing. The original text stated that it 'does not in any way constitute a binding commitment'. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the original intent of the clause and creates an in-text contradiction, which is supported by the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding termination, the obligations of limited use and nondisclosure under this Agreement shall remain in effect until expiration of the period referred to in Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding termination, the obligations of limited use and nondisclosure under this Agreement shall remain in effect until expiration of the period referred to in Section 2, except in the case of termination due to gross negligence by the Disclosing Party, in which case all obligations are immediately void.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause in Section 11 creates a contradiction. The original sentence states that confidentiality obligations remain in effect even after termination. The added exception now states if the termination is because of gross negligence by the disclosing party then that obligation is void. This creates contradiction because you don't know in which situation, you would be in.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement by providing written notice to the other party. Notwithstanding termination, the obligations of limited use and nondisclosure under this Agreement shall remain in effect until expiration of the period referred to in Section 2, except in the case of termination due to gross negligence by the Disclosing Party, in which case all obligations are immediately void.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original text regarding termination due to gross negligence by the disclosing party. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case. The original text says the confidentiality obligations remain in effect even after termination. The added exception now states if the termination is because of gross negligence by the disclosing party then that obligation is void. This creates a direct contradiction because now the enforcement of the confidentiality obligations is conditional and depends on the cause of termination, which creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may only be amended by written agreement of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may only be amended by written agreement of both parties, unless one party notifies the other of changes. This will be considered accepted without needing the other parties approval.
perturbation.explanation: Section 12, establishes that, to make any amendment, it must be done with agreement of both parties. Adding the clause "unless one party notifies the other of changes. This will be considered accepted without needing the other parties approval." introduces a clear contradiction, as this is against the original statement, where both parties have to agree to amend the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Amendment; Entire Agreement. This Agreement may only be amended by written agreement of both parties, unless one party notifies the other of changes. This will be considered accepted without needing the other parties approval. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding confidentiality and use of Confidential Information disclosed in connection with the Discussions or the Negotiations. This Agreement supersedes all previous oral and written agreements, if any, between the parties regarding the confidentiality of information disclosed in connection with the Discussions or the Negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement, which required both parties to agree on any amendments. The added clause allows one party to unilaterally amend the agreement, directly conflicting with the initial condition. The Contradicted Text section contains the exact language that is being contradicted, strengthening the case for a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.
